novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1
novelty_flags.one_line: The derivative-target literature comparison misses Zhang2025, which gives sensitivity bounds for the average derivative effect under continuous exposures; it does not subsume the rank-slice IV/copula kernel, but it is a closer derivative comparator that must be distinguished.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add Zhang2025 as a derivative-target comparator and state explicitly why ADE sensitivity bounds do not answer the baseline-rank-slice adjacent finite-difference sign question under IV-compatible copula tubes.

Rename or sharpen the 'one-dose rank-slice marginal endpoints' definition to make clear that the a1 endpoint conditions on the pinned baseline rank via an unrestricted cross-dose coupling, not a literal one-dose-only model.

Add a small symbolic two-bin witness table in §9 or §10 with row/column margins and the signed IV moment inequality, so the phase-transition conjecture has a concrete algebraic target rather than only a route description.

recommended_next_step: Revise the literature comparison around continuous-treatment derivative sensitivity, especially Zhang2025, while preserving the IV/copula rank-slice novelty kernel.
